description: Conformance vectors for the Graph Advocate counterparty reputation provider. Demonstrates that POST /agent/score returns a counterparty-ref-v1 conformant SHA-256 hex pointer over the canonical JCS preimage. Each accept vector carries the five-field preimage (provider_id, rubric_version, timestamp, trailing_days, wallet), the RFC 8785 JCS canonical form, and the lowercase-hex SHA-256. The reject vector exercises the timestamp-omission failure mode from the provider side.

vectors.description: Graph Advocate self-score (ERC-8004 agent #41034 on Base). 90-day trailing window, midnight UTC anchor.
vectors.notes: Live endpoint POST https://graphadvocate.com/agent/score returns the same preimage + counterparty_ref when called with this wallet + trailing_days at the recorded timestamp (subject to 24h cache TTL).

vectors.description: Wallet-behavior-score peer agent (owner of 12 ERC-8004-registered agents on Base, latest #55656). 30-day trailing window, midnight UTC anchor. Demonstrates owner-level aggregation: feedback accrues to whichever agent users hit (here #51123, not the latest), so the per-wallet score aggregates across the owner's full agent set.
vectors.notes: Same provider_id and rubric_version as ga-001 but different (wallet, trailing_days, timestamp) triple produces a distinct ref — multi-vector confirmation that the preimage components all contribute.

vectors.description: Reject: preimage missing the timestamp field. counterparty-ref-v1 §'Why timestamp is required': without timestamp the hash is floating and cannot satisfy the anchor requirement. A spec-conformant verifier MUST refuse to hash this rather than substitute a default value.
vectors.notes: Companion to giskard09/argentum-core examples/conformance/counterparty-ref/reject-missing-timestamp.json — same failure mode, exercised from the provider-side (graph-advocate).
